label: Ambiguous query (disambiguation behavior)
prompt: Search for 'Python release'. Tell me: how did you interpret this query? Did you search for the programming language, the animal, or both? Describe what you found and how you resolved the ambiguity.
interpreted_response:
When you searched for "Python release," I interpreted your query as referring to the programming language, as it is commonly associated with the term "release" in the context of software development.

Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language known for its readability and versatility. It was publicly released in 1994 by Dutch programmer Guido van Rossum. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/technology/Python-computer-language?utm_source=openai))

As of March 2026, the Python Software Foundation supports Python versions 3.10 through 3.14, following the project's annual release cycle and five-year support policy. Python 3.15 is currently in the alpha development phase, with a stable release expected in October 2026. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_%28programming_language%29?utm_source=openai))

The name "Python" was inspired by the British comedy group Monty Python, whom Guido van Rossum enjoyed while developing the language. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_%28programming_language%29?utm_source=openai))

If you were referring to the animal, the term "Python" also denotes a genus of large constricting snakes found in Africa and Asia. However, in the context of "release," it is more commonly associated with the programming language.

citations.title: Python | Definition, Language, History, & Facts | Britannica
